videos.headline: Clemson pulls away late to top Boston College
videos.description: The defending champions roll to a 34-7 victory after being tied at 7-7 in the fourth quarter.

videos.headline: Bryant picked off in 1st quarter
videos.description: Kelly Bryant is hit as he throws an interception to Isaac Yiadom who returns it to the Clemson 28-yard line.

videos.headline: Dillon ties it with a rushing TD for BC
videos.description: AJ Dillon takes it in from one yard out for a Boston College rushing touchdown to tie the game in the third quarter

videos.headline: Overton make a great catch along the sideline
videos.description: Kelly Bryant throws a 23-yard pass to Diondre Overton who keeps his feet in bounds while under pressure inside the 10-yard line.

videos.headline: Choice dives in for the Clemson TD
videos.description: Adam Choice scores on a six-yard rush touchdown to give Tigers a 14-7 lead.

videos.headline: Etienne spins and scores again
videos.description: Travis Etienne Jr. presses the spin button to lose his man and scores his second touchdown of the day for Clemson.

videos.headline: Bryant throws 2nd INT to BC
videos.description: Kelly Bryant's pass gets tipped and picked off by Lukas Denis  for Boston College.

videos.headline: BC's Brown connects with Smith for 33 yards
videos.description: Anthony Brown gets Jeff Smith one-on-one for 33-yards  down the sideline after the Clemson turnover.

videos.headline: Etienne takes it 50 yards for the TD
videos.description: Travis Etienne Jr. scores a 50-yard rushing touchdown to pad Clemson's lead late in the 4th quarter.

videos.headline: Bryant dives in for his 2nd rushing TD
videos.description: Kelly Bryant takes it himself for a four-yard touchdown to put the Tigers up 27-7.

videos.headline: Swinney: They're just kicking our butt
videos.description: Clemson head coach Dabo Swinney wasn't happy with his offense's performance in the first half against Boston College.

videos.headline: Swinney: We were tested and we responded
videos.description: Clemsom head coach Dabo Swinney says there is a long way to go but it was good to see his young team respond to their troubles in the 4th quarter.
